summary: Peace talks between the U.S. and Iran collapsed over the weekend, and Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, sending oil surging and equities lower. The effective closure of the key shipping route has driven energy prices sharply higher and heightened inflation risks, reinforcing expectations that central banks may delay rate cuts or even tighten policy further.
tweet: U.S.-Iran talks collapsed and Trump blockaded the Strait of Hormuz — stocks dropped over 1% at the open as banks kick off earnings season under a darkening macro backdrop.
